name: Khiva
description: Khiva in Khorezm Region is a 2,500-year-old “open-air museum” city, UNESCO-listed Itchan Kala with madrasas, mosques and minarets (e.g. Kalta Minor). A key Silk Road cultural and trade hub; inner town is a dense ensemble of medieval monuments among the best preserved in Central Asia.

monuments.title: Kalta Minor
monuments.info: Unfinished minaret before Muhammad Amin Khan Madrasa, clad in blue tiles — Khiva’s emblem; one of the tallest medieval minarets in Uzbekistan.

monuments.title: Friday Mosque
monuments.info: 213 carved wooden columns support the roof; 10th–18th c. mosque as a vast hypostyle hall in the centre of Itchan Kala.

monuments.title: Kunya Ark
monuments.info: 17th c. (1686–1688) citadel rebuilt in the 19th c.: reception halls, mosque, mint, harem and prison — seat of Khivan khans.

monuments.title: Pahlavan Mahmud complex
monuments.info: Shrine to poet, philosopher and wrestler Pahlavan Mahmud (1247–1326); blue dome and tilework; burial place of Khorezm rulers.

monuments.title: Islam Khoja minaret
monuments.info: 1908–1910, tallest and newest minaret of Itchan Kala (~56 m), built for vizier Islam Khoja; tapering shaft with glazed patterns.

monuments.title: Tash Hovli Palace
monuments.info: Allakuli Khan’s palace (111 rooms: court of justice, pleasure court, harem) with blue tiles and carved wooden columns.

monuments.title: Muhammad Amin Khan Madrasa
monuments.info: 1852–1855 by the west gate of Itchan Kala; two storeys, 125 cells; among the largest madrasas; now Orient Star Khiva hotel.

monuments.title: Nurullaboy Palace
monuments.info: Former official reception palace of Asfandiyarkhan in Dishan Kala; summer residence blending Eastern and European styles.

monuments.title: Allakuli Khan Madrasa
monuments.info: 1834–1835 by Polvon Gate on an artificial platform; khanaka, caravanserai and covered bazaar form one ensemble.

monuments.title: Shergazi Khan Madrasa
monuments.info: 1719–1726, “abode of scholars” near Pahlavan Mahmud’s entrance.

monuments.title: Sayid Alauddin Mausoleum
monuments.info: 14th c. over Sheikh Alauddin’s grave; fine glazed tomb chamber — high Timurid-era art.

monuments.title: Ota Darvoza (West Gate)
monuments.info: Main western gate into Itchan Kala, rebuilt 1970s; leads to Kunya Ark and Muhammad Amin Khan Madrasa.

monuments.title: Polvon Darvoza (East Gate)
monuments.info: 1806 eastern gate, busiest entrance; iwans and bazaar rows toward Allakuli Khan Madrasa.

monuments.title: Tosh Darvoza (South Gate)
monuments.info: 1830s–40s southern gate of Itchan Kala; stone-built, controlled trade routes to the outer town.

monuments.title: Bogcha Darvoza (North Gate)
monuments.info: 19th c. northern gate with six domed chambers; main approach from Urgench.

monuments.title: Kutlug Murad Inaq Madrasa
monuments.info: Early 19th c. madrasa of the khan’s uncle; among the first two-storey cell designs and an underground cistern (sardoba).
